source.notes: Production PR implementing LLM API circuit breaker with WAIT/CAP/STOP 429 classification for Claude backend. 1,631 lines of new code, 62 tests. PR description explicitly credits @SirBrenton for the classification taxonomy. Implemented within 4 hours of comment on issue #1014.

hazard.summary: LLM API circuit breaker conflates WAIT, CAP, and STOP 429 cases — quota exhaustion treated as retryable, burning budget and triggering retry storms in multi-agent orchestration
signals: 429 with short Retry-After (<= 60s) → WAIT: sleep and retry, no CB failure counted
429 with long Retry-After (> 60s) → STOP: force-open circuit breaker, no retry
429 with no Retry-After → CAP: backoff and retry with reduced concurrency, CB failure counted
Claude, ChatCompletions, Response API had zero 429 detection or classification before this PR
Gemini had retry logic but no WAIT/CAP/STOP distinction

constraints: STOP cases must force-open the circuit breaker immediately — not retry
WAIT cases must not increment the CB failure counter — they are recoverable
CAP cases must reduce concurrency before retry — not just delay
Classification must run before any enforcement decision
retry_after_threshold_seconds must be configurable — default 60s separates WAIT from STOP
knobs: retry_after_threshold_seconds — default 60 (429 with Retry-After above this → STOP)
max_failures — default 5 (CB opens after N consecutive failures)
enabled — default false (opt-in, no behavior change until explicitly enabled)
verification: 429 with Retry-After: 30 → WAIT classification, CB stays CLOSED
429 with Retry-After: 120 → STOP classification, CB transitions to OPEN immediately
429 with no Retry-After → CAP classification, CB failure counter increments
Concurrent access: exactly-one probe in HALF_OPEN state
notes: This receipt documents the first external adoption of the WAIT/CAP/STOP taxonomy as a production implementation. The PR credits @SirBrenton explicitly and was implemented within 4 hours of a comment on issue #1014. This is not a bug report — it is a production feature built around the taxonomy. Phase 1 covers Claude backend only. Phases 2 and 3 will extend to ChatCompletions, Response API, and Gemini.
